original: Henry I becomes Count of Leuven

Lord Deputy of Ireland Thomas Wentworth is recalled to England
Austro-Ottoman War: Battle of Vienna - several European armies join forces to defeat the Ottoman Empire
New York Jews petition governor Dongan for religious liberties
Emperor Leopold I ends contacts on Spanish heritage
English army under Arch Duke Charles of Austria lands in Portugal
Isaak of Hoornbeek elected Dutch pension advisor
Russian troops occupy Baku & Derbent in Persia
Polish Landowners select Stanislaw Lesczynski king
Amsterdam refuses establishment of Jewish ghetto
British soldiers capture the town of Quebec
Battle of North Point fought near Baltimore during War of 1812
Moreton Bay Penal Settlement established at Redcliffe, Queensland, with about 30 convicts (modern Brisbane, Australia)

HMS Terror and HMS Erebus, the British expedition searching for a Northwest Passage, led by John Franklin, become trapped by ice near King William Island (all eventually perish)
Mexican–American War: the Battle of Chapultepec begins
Switzerland becomes a Federal state
2nd synagogue in Curacao, Emanu-El of Willemstad, inaugurated

Cleopatra Needle installed in London
Highest football score recorded in any 1st-class soccer match: Arbroath 36–0 Bon Accord in Scotland
Annie Londonderry [Annie Kopchovsky] arrives in Chicago to complete first round-the-world trip by a woman on a bicycle in 15 months and collects her $10,000 prize
Defender (US) beats Valkyrie III (England) in 10th America's Cup

Battle of Saragarhi: Thousands of Orakzai and Afridi tribesmen overwhelm and kill at great cost to themselves 21 British Raj Sikh Soldiers led by Havildar Ishar Singh at Tirah, North-West Frontier Province, British India
Arabs attack Gedara Palestine
The Newport Transporter Bridge is opened in Newport, South Wales by Viscount Tredegar.
Canada appoints a Civil Service Commission, initiating a more equitable system for selecting civil servants
World's first patent for synthetic rubber granted to German chemist Fritz Hofmann

Dutch Olympian Committee forms (NOC)
Yankee shortstop Roger Peckinpaugh, 23, becomes youngest manager
WWI: US forces launch an attack on German-occupied St Mihiel
VII Summer Olympic Games close at the Olympisch Stadion in Antwerp, Belgium
Britain takes over Southern Rhodesia from British South Africa Co.
Sigmund Romberg's musical "My Maryland," premieres in NYC

Inaugural Bledisloe Cup rugby match played between Australia and New Zealand at Eden Park, Auckland - NZ win 20-13
Brooklyn Dodger Johnny Frederick hits his record 6th pinch hit HR
German government of Papen falls and the Reichstag dissolved
Alejandro Lerroux forms new Spanish government
Dutch parliament accepts ban on uniforms
"L'Atalante", French film directed by Jean Vigo, starring Michel Simon, Dita Parlo and Jean Dasté, is released

49 die & 200 injured when Hercules Powder Co plant explodes u in New Jersey

Free-Poland & Belgium asks pope to condemn nazi-war crimes

US Army troops enter Germany for 1st time

Belgian government dismisses all communist civil servants
Brooklyn Dodgers clinch NL pennant earlier than any other team, defeating Milwaukee Braves 5-2. Brooklyn finishes season with record of 105-49.

MLB Cleveland Indians sweep New York Yankees at home (Municipal Stadium) before largest American League crowd (86,563)

Black students enter & are barred from Clay Ky elementary school
Archbishop Makarios of Cyprus visits US
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Janos Kádár becomes premier of Hungary
Luna 2 is launched by the Soviet Union, the first spacecraft to impact the Moon

1st football game at Shea Stadium, Jets defeat Denver 30-6

2 bettors win largest US Daily Double ($19,909.60 in Detroit)

Flight readiness firing of Atlantis' main engines; 20 seconds

Gilbert, strongest hurricane ever (160 mph), devastates Jamaica

The bodies of more than 40 patients discovered in a flooded hospital in New Orleans.
The red-green coalition, led by Jens Stoltenberg, wins the Norwegian parliamentary election, taking 87 of 169 seats in the parliament.
Glenn Beck's 9-12 Project organizes multiple marches and demonstrations across the USA to protest government spending

South Africa claims 3rd Tri Nations Rugby Series with 32-29 win over defending champions New Zealand in Hamilton; Steyn bros dominate with the boot; Morné kicks 2 penalties, 2 conversions & dropped goal; François, 3 penalties
